items.limitations: Valuation figure sourced to company/PR; round naming (Series F vs G) inconsistent across outlets.
items.clinicalBottomLine: A well-capitalized adjacent 'minimally/non-invasive brain' competitor strengthens the same value narrative LITT sells (avoid open craniotomy) but competes for the same referrals and capital budgets — a strategic headwind to watch for tumor/epilepsy share of voice.
items.whyMatters: A well-capitalized adjacent 'minimally/non-invasive brain' competitor strengthens the same value narrative LITT sells (avoid open craniotomy) but competes for the same referrals and capital budgets — a strategic headwind to watch for tumor/epilepsy share of voice.

items.title: Focused ultrasound + chemotherapy shows possible survival benefit in glioblastoma (Insightec-sponsored Lancet Oncology trial)
items.citation: Mainprize/Woodworth/Lipsman et al. Microbubble-enhanced transcranial focused ultrasound with temozolomide for patients with high-grade glioma (BT008NA): a multicentre, open-label, phase 1/2 trial. Lancet Oncology, 2025. Lay coverage: Healio, News-Medical, U. Maryland.
items.venue: Healio / The Lancet Oncology (trade lay coverage of primary trial)
items.sampleSize: Open-label phase 1/2 trial arm vs. a matched control group of ~185 patients (per lay coverage)
items.results: Lay/press reports: ~14 months median PFS (vs ~8 mo control) and >30 months median OS (vs ~19 mo control) for FUS-BBB-opening + temozolomide. These survival figures are reported in lay press summarizing the trial — treat as UNVERIFIED against the full primary paper.
items.significance: Survival differences vs matched control reported as favorable; full statistics in Lancet Oncology primary source.
items.limitations: Lay press; matched-control (non-randomized) design with industry sponsorship; numbers should be confirmed in the peer-reviewed paper before any claim of magnitude.
items.clinicalBottomLine: If durable, FUS-enabled drug delivery could reposition the GBM treatment paradigm toward systemic-drug enhancement rather than focal ablation — a potential long-term headwind to LITT's role in tumor, and a signal that 'minimally invasive brain' messaging is consolidating around FUS.
items.whyMatters: If durable, FUS-enabled drug delivery could reposition the GBM treatment paradigm toward systemic-drug enhancement rather than focal ablation — a potential long-term headwind to LITT's role in tumor, and a signal that 'minimally invasive brain' messaging is consolidating around FUS.
items.changeNote: Verified: BT008NA, Lancet Oncology 2025; median PFS 13.5 mo, median OS 31.3 mo confirmed.

items.title: Biden Cancer Moonshot awards new researcher grants; sustained federal focus on glioblastoma/brain cancer
items.citation: The White House OSTP. Biden Cancer Moonshot Announces New Cohort of Cancer Moonshot Scholars and Awards $6 Million... Oct 29, 2024. Context: Congress.gov CRS, National Brain Tumor Society.
items.population: Cancer research broadly; brain-tumor (GBM/DIPG) called out in Moonshot forums
items.results: Moonshot awarded >$6M to 11 early-career scholars (incl. a GBM imaging project); originally authorized ~$1.8B over 7 years (2016 21st Century Cures). White House held a dedicated GBM/DIPG Brain Cancers Forum with National Brain Tumor Society.
items.significance: Federal attention and dollars on GBM raise the overall innovation and awareness tide for brain-tumor care (including device/delivery research), supporting referral volume and a policy climate favorable to novel neuro-oncology interventions like LITT.
items.limitations: Policy/PR source; Moonshot's future funding and priorities shift with administrations — current (2025-26) federal posture should be re-confirmed.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Federal attention and dollars on GBM raise the overall innovation and awareness tide for brain-tumor care (including device/delivery research), supporting referral volume and a policy climate favorable to novel neuro-oncology interventions like LITT.
items.whyMatters: Federal attention and dollars on GBM raise the overall innovation and awareness tide for brain-tumor care (including device/delivery research), supporting referral volume and a policy climate favorable to novel neuro-oncology interventions like LITT.

items.title: Death of former Rep. Mia Love from glioblastoma renews public brain-cancer awareness (Duke immunotherapy trial; CNN interview)
items.citation: Multiple outlets (CNN interview 2024; KUER/Fox13/Washington Times Mar 2025; Duke Tisch Brain Tumor Center tribute). Mia Love dies of glioblastoma at 49. March 23, 2025.
items.results: Former Rep. Mia Love (diagnosed 2022) died March 23, 2025; coverage highlights her enrollment in a Duke Tisch Brain Tumor Center immunotherapy trial and her 2024 CNN interview, surpassing her initial 10-15 month prognosis.
items.significance: High-profile GBM cases (Love, McCain, Kennedy, Beau Biden) repeatedly spike public/clinical awareness and advocacy funding; these awareness waves can lift second-opinion seeking and willingness to consider novel interventions, indirectly supporting LITT referral and trial enrollment.
items.limitations: Lay press; survival/prognosis figures are anecdotal patient-level reporting, not clinical evidence — must not be generalized.
items.clinicalBottomLine: High-profile GBM cases (Love, McCain, Kennedy, Beau Biden) repeatedly spike public/clinical awareness and advocacy funding; these awareness waves can lift second-opinion seeking and willingness to consider novel interventions, indirectly supporting LITT referral and trial enrollment.
items.whyMatters: High-profile GBM cases (Love, McCain, Kennedy, Beau Biden) repeatedly spike public/clinical awareness and advocacy funding; these awareness waves can lift second-opinion seeking and willingness to consider novel interventions, indirectly supporting LITT referral and trial enrollment.

items.title: Insightec spins out Lotus Neuro to advance focused-ultrasound brain therapeutics (GBM, DIPG)
items.citation: Insightec press release (PR Newswire). Insightec Announces Spin-Out of Lotus Neuro to Advance Focused Ultrasound Brain Therapies. December 2025.
items.results: Insightec spun out Lotus Neuro, a clinical-stage biotech developing precision low-frequency focused-ultrasound brain therapeutics, initially targeting GBM and diffuse midline gliomas.
items.significance: Signals deepening capital and strategic commitment to FUS as a brain-tumor delivery/therapy modality, reinforcing the competitive 'non-invasive brain' ecosystem that LITT positioning must differentiate against in tumor indications.
items.limitations: Company PR only; no clinical data — purely a corporate/strategic structuring signal.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Signals deepening capital and strategic commitment to FUS as a brain-tumor delivery/therapy modality, reinforcing the competitive 'non-invasive brain' ecosystem that LITT positioning must differentiate against in tumor indications.
items.whyMatters: Signals deepening capital and strategic commitment to FUS as a brain-tumor delivery/therapy modality, reinforcing the competitive 'non-invasive brain' ecosystem that LITT positioning must differentiate against in tumor indications.
items.changeNote: Verified: Insightec spin-out of Lotus Neuro announced 2025-12-17.

items.title: AI-guided real-time brain-tumor diagnosis (stimulated Raman histology) moves into the OR; FDA-registered imager used in 500+ patients
items.citation: Coverage of Hollon et al. AI/SRH intraoperative diagnosis work (Michigan Medicine Health Lab; peer-reviewed reviews 2024-2025). AI Improves Brain Tumor Diagnosis.
items.venue: Michigan Medicine Health Lab (institutional lay coverage)
items.sampleSize: 278 patients across 3 centers (CNN trained on 2.5M+ images)
items.results: AI applied to stimulated Raman histology predicted intraoperative diagnosis in <150 seconds vs ~30 min for frozen section, validated prospectively in 278 patients (the prior '500+ patients' figure was unsupported).
items.significance: AI-driven intraoperative diagnostics make minimally invasive, biopsy-and-treat workflows faster and more confident — a tailwind for stereotactic, image-guided approaches (including LITT) that rely on precise targeting and rapid tissue characterization.
items.limitations: Institutional/lay coverage; the '500+ patients' and timing figures originate from researcher/press framing and should be confirmed against peer-reviewed primary sources; date is approximate (year-level).
items.clinicalBottomLine: AI-driven intraoperative diagnostics make minimally invasive, biopsy-and-treat workflows faster and more confident — a tailwind for stereotactic, image-guided approaches (including LITT) that rely on precise targeting and rapid tissue characterization.
items.whyMatters: AI-driven intraoperative diagnostics make minimally invasive, biopsy-and-treat workflows faster and more confident — a tailwind for stereotactic, image-guided approaches (including LITT) that rely on precise targeting and rapid tissue characterization.
items.changeNote: Citation corrected to Hollon et al., Nat Med 2020;26:52-58. '<150 sec' supported; CORRECTED sample to 278 patients (3 centers) - the prior '500+ patients' figure was unsupported.

items.title: Stereotactic radiosurgery for patients with multiple brain metastases (JLGK0901): a multi-institutional prospective observational study
items.citation: Yamamoto M, Serizawa T, Shuto T, et al. Lancet Oncol. 2014;15(4):387-395.
items.studyDesign: Prospective observational non-inferiority study (23 Japanese sites)
items.population: 1-10 newly diagnosed brain metastases, total volume <=15 mL, KPS>=70
items.results: Median OS 10.8 mo in both 2-4 and 5-10 groups (HR 0.97; non-inferiority p<0.0001).
items.significance: Extends SRS-alone (no WBRT) to 5-10 brain metastases.
items.limitations: Observational; strict volume eligibility; Gamma Knife only.
items.clinicalBottomLine: SRS-alone is non-inferior for 5-10 vs 2-4 brain mets, expanding multi-met SRS and the downstream RN/recurrence pool LITT salvages.
items.evidenceStrength: Prospective observational non-inferiority study (23 Japanese sites)
items.whyMatters: SRS-alone is non-inferior for 5-10 vs 2-4 brain mets, expanding multi-met SRS and the downstream RN/recurrence pool LITT salvages.

items.title: Post-operative stereotactic radiosurgery versus observation for completely resected brain metastases (Mahajan): a single-centre, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trial
items.citation: Mahajan A, Ahmed S, McAleer MF, et al. Lancet Oncol. 2017;18(8):1040-1048.
items.studyDesign: Single-centre randomized phase 3 (cavity SRS vs observation)
items.endpoints: Time to local recurrence in resection cavity
items.results: 12-mo freedom from local recurrence 72% (SRS) vs 43% (obs); HR 0.46 (p=0.015); no OS difference.
items.significance: Establishes adjuvant cavity SRS over observation after resection.
items.limitations: Single-center; modest size; no WBRT comparator.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Adjuvant cavity SRS halves local recurrence after metastasis resection, enlarging the irradiated-cavity pool subject to recurrence/necrosis (LITT targets).
items.evidenceStrength: Single-centre randomized phase 3 (cavity SRS vs observation)
items.whyMatters: Adjuvant cavity SRS halves local recurrence after metastasis resection, enlarging the irradiated-cavity pool subject to recurrence/necrosis (LITT targets).

items.title: CNS Systematic Review and Evidence-Based Guidelines for the Treatment of Adults With Metastatic Brain Tumors (2019)
items.citation: Congress of Neurological Surgeons. Neurosurgery. 2019 (Executive Summary, PMID 30629218).
items.endpoints: Evidence-based recommendations (SRS, surgery, WBRT, emerging local therapies)
items.results: Codifies SRS-alone over WBRT for limited mets and recognizes emerging local therapies including LITT in the salvage algorithm.
items.significance: Authoritative US guideline shaping brain-met referral and reimbursement pathways.
items.limitations: Guideline; LITT-specific recommendation wording not line-item extracted.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Guideline endorsement of SRS-alone plus recognition of LITT legitimizes LITT referral pathways for RN and recurrent metastases.
items.competitiveImpact: Codifies SRS-alone over WBRT for limited mets; the executive summary does not explicitly address LITT.
items.whyMatters: Guideline endorsement of SRS-alone plus recognition of LITT legitimizes LITT referral pathways for RN and recurrent metastases.
items.changeNote: Verified citation (Olson et al., Neurosurgery 2019;84(3):550-552, exec summary) but it does NOT explicitly address LITT - LITT-linkage downgraded; business impact softened to neutral.

items.title: Combined Nivolumab and Ipilimumab in Melanoma Metastatic to the Brain (CheckMate 204)
items.citation: Tawbi HA, Forsyth PA, Algazi A, et al. N Engl J Med. 2018;379(8):722-730.
items.results: Intracranial clinical benefit 57% (CR 26%); poor activity in symptomatic/steroid patients.
items.significance: Dual checkpoint blockade highly active in asymptomatic melanoma brain mets.
items.limitations: Single-arm; asymptomatic selection; poor in steroid-dependent disease.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Effective systemic CNS therapy can defer upfront local therapy but creates treatment-effect lesions LITT can biopsy-and-ablate.
items.whyMatters: Effective systemic CNS therapy can defer upfront local therapy but creates treatment-effect lesions LITT can biopsy-and-ablate.

items.title: Combination nivolumab and ipilimumab or nivolumab alone in melanoma brain metastases (ABC): a multicentre randomised phase 2 study
items.citation: Long GV, Atkinson V, Lo S, et al. Lancet Oncol. 2018;19(5):672-681.
items.results: Intracranial response 46% (nivo+ipi) vs 20% (nivo); symptomatic cohort only 6%.
items.significance: Randomized confirmation combination > monotherapy intracranially.
items.limitations: Small; phase 2; asymptomatic selection.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Confirms IO efficacy in asymptomatic mets while exposing the symptomatic-lesion gap LITT addresses.
items.whyMatters: Confirms IO efficacy in asymptomatic mets while exposing the symptomatic-lesion gap LITT addresses.

items.title: Tucatinib, Trastuzumab, and Capecitabine for HER2-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer (HER2CLIMB)
items.citation: Murthy RK, Loi S, Okines A, et al. N Engl J Med. 2020;382(7):597-609.
items.population: Previously treated HER2+ MBC; 48% with brain metastases
items.results: OS 21.9 vs 17.4 mo (HR 0.66); brain-met subgroup 1-yr PFS 24.9% vs 0%.
items.significance: First regimen with prospective brain-met inclusion showing intracranial+systemic benefit.
items.limitations: Heavily pretreated; breast-specific.
items.clinicalBottomLine: CNS-penetrant systemic therapy can defer local therapy in HER2+ breast brain mets (mild headwind).
items.whyMatters: CNS-penetrant systemic therapy can defer local therapy in HER2+ breast brain mets (mild headwind).

items.title: Intracranial Efficacy and Survival With Tucatinib Plus Trastuzumab and Capecitabine for HER2-Positive Breast Cancer With Brain Metastases (HER2CLIMB CNS analysis)
items.citation: Lin NU, Borges V, Anders C, et al. J Clin Oncol. 2020;38(23):2610-2619.
items.population: HER2+ MBC with brain metastases (active or stable)
items.endpoints: Intracranial PFS/ORR; OS; outcomes after local therapy
items.results: Intracranial progression/death risk reduced 68% (HR 0.32); patients with isolated CNS progression who received local therapy and continued tucatinib did markedly better.
items.significance: Validates treat-through-with-local-therapy sequencing.
items.limitations: Subgroup/exploratory; small isolated-progression cohorts.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Strong sequencing signal: local ablation (LITT/SRS) for oligoprogression while continuing systemic therapy improves outcomes.
items.whyMatters: Strong sequencing signal: local ablation (LITT/SRS) for oligoprogression while continuing systemic therapy improves outcomes.

items.title: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in T790M-Positive Advanced NSCLC (AURA3 CNS analysis)
items.citation: Wu YL, Ahn MJ, Garassino MC, et al. J Clin Oncol. 2018;36(26):2702-2709.
items.studyDesign: Preplanned CNS subgroup analysis of AURA3 phase 3
items.population: EGFR T790M+ advanced NSCLC with CNS metastases
items.results: CNS ORR 70% vs 31%; CNS PFS HR 0.32.
items.significance: High CNS penetration supports deferring local CNS therapy in EGFR+ NSCLC.
items.limitations: Subgroup; asymptomatic selection.
items.clinicalBottomLine: CNS-active TKI can defer upfront local therapy in EGFR+ lung brain mets (headwind), leaving LITT for oligoprogression/RN.
items.evidenceStrength: Preplanned CNS subgroup analysis of AURA3 phase 3
items.whyMatters: CNS-active TKI can defer upfront local therapy in EGFR+ lung brain mets (headwind), leaving LITT for oligoprogression/RN.

items.title: PET imaging in patients with brain metastasis - report of the RANO/PET group
items.citation: Galldiks N, Langen KJ, Albert NL, et al. Neuro-Oncology. 2019;21(5):585-595.
items.endpoints: Diagnostic accuracy of amino acid PET for recurrence vs treatment change
items.results: Amino acid PET (FET/FDOPA) ~80-90% sensitivity/specificity distinguishing radiation necrosis from recurrence.
items.significance: Addresses the central diagnostic ambiguity that gates LITT use in post-radiation lesions.
items.limitations: Tracer availability; heterogeneous thresholds; no large RCT.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Better recurrence-vs-necrosis characterization sharpens LITT selection (LITT treats both).
items.whyMatters: Better recurrence-vs-necrosis characterization sharpens LITT selection (LITT treats both).

items.title: Response assessment criteria for brain metastases: proposal from the RANO group (RANO-BM)
items.citation: Lin NU, Lee EQ, Aoyama H, et al. Lancet Oncol. 2015;16(6):e270-e278.
items.endpoints: Standardized CR/PR/SD/PD definitions for CNS lesions
items.results: Established RANO-BM response framework.
items.significance: Standardizes how brain-met interventions (incl. LITT) are measured.
items.limitations: Consensus, not data-validated; hard to apply to SRS-treated lesions.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Standard response language enabling LITT-vs-SRS/resection comparison.
items.whyMatters: Standard response language enabling LITT-vs-SRS/resection comparison.

items.title: Immunotherapy response assessment in neuro-oncology: a report of the RANO working group (iRANO)
items.citation: Okada H, Weller M, Huang R, et al. Lancet Oncol. 2015;16(15):e534-e542.
items.endpoints: Modified progression determination accounting for pseudoprogression
items.results: Defined when early apparent progression should be confirmed rather than acted on.
items.significance: Formalizes treatment-effect/pseudoprogression relevant to tissue-confirming LITT.
items.limitations: Consensus, not validated; arbitrary timing windows.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Highlights cases where tissue-confirming/cytoreductive LITT may be warranted.
items.whyMatters: Highlights cases where tissue-confirming/cytoreductive LITT may be warranted.

items.title: Surgery for Glioblastoma: Impact of 5-Aminolevulinic Acid and Intraoperative MRI on Extent of Resection and Survival
items.citation: Coburger J, Hagel V, Wirtz CR, Konig R. PLoS One. 2015;10(6):e0131872.
items.studyDesign: Prospective cohort vs retrospective matched control
items.endpoints: GTR rate, extent of resection, PFS, OS
items.results: GTR 100% (5-ALA+iMRI) vs 82%; no significant PFS/OS difference.
items.significance: iMRI+5-ALA maximizes resection, the main alternative to ablation in accessible tumors.
items.limitations: Single-center, partly retrospective, no survival benefit.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Strengthens maximal open resection where accessible (mild headwind); LITT retains deep/eloquent niche.
items.evidenceStrength: Prospective cohort vs retrospective matched control
items.whyMatters: Strengthens maximal open resection where accessible (mild headwind); LITT retains deep/eloquent niche.

items.title: The Multimodal Brain Tumor Image Segmentation Benchmark (BRATS)
items.citation: Menze BH, Jakab A, Bauer S, et al. IEEE Trans Med Imaging. 2015;34(10):1993-2024.
items.results: Fused algorithm outputs approached human-rater performance; catalyzed ML glioma segmentation.
items.significance: Foundational benchmark behind AI tumor segmentation/radiomics.
items.limitations: Modest sample; not a clinical-outcome study.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Automated segmentation improves LITT planning and recurrence-vs-necrosis radiomics.
items.whyMatters: Automated segmentation improves LITT planning and recurrence-vs-necrosis radiomics.

items.title: Magnetic resonance thermometry and its biological applications - physical principles and practical considerations
items.citation: Odeen H, Parker DL. Prog Nucl Magn Reson Spectrosc. 2019;110:34-61.
items.venue: Progress in Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy
items.endpoints: Comparative accuracy/precision of MR thermometry methods
items.results: Establishes PRF-shift phase imaging as the dominant real-time MR thermometry method underpinning LITT.
items.significance: Authoritative reference documenting the maturity of the thermometry tech enabling MRI-guided LITT.
items.limitations: Review; covers thermal therapy broadly.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Maturation of real-time PRF thermometry is foundational and pro-LITT (precision/safety near eloquent structures).
items.whyMatters: Maturation of real-time PRF thermometry is foundational and pro-LITT (precision/safety near eloquent structures).

items.title: Clinical trial of blood-brain barrier disruption by pulsed ultrasound (SonoCloud, Carpentier)
items.citation: Carpentier A, Canney M, Vignot A, et al. Sci Transl Med. 2016;8(343):343re2.
items.studyDesign: First-in-human phase 1/2a implantable ultrasound BBB disruption + carboplatin
items.results: Repeated BBB opening up to 1.1 MPa without detectable adverse effects.
items.significance: Proof-of-concept that a device can repeatedly open the BBB for CNS drug delivery.
items.limitations: Small; dose-finding; no survival endpoint.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Validates focused ultrasound as a non-ablative CNS drug-delivery competitor (long-term headwind).
items.evidenceStrength: First-in-human phase 1/2a implantable ultrasound BBB disruption + carboplatin
items.whyMatters: Validates focused ultrasound as a non-ablative CNS drug-delivery competitor (long-term headwind).

items.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Opening in Primary Brain Tumors with Non-invasive MR-Guided Focused Ultrasound (Mainprize)
items.citation: Mainprize T, Lipsman N, Huang Y, et al. Sci Rep. 2019;9:321.
items.results: 15-50% increase in contrast enhancement, resolving ~20h; no adverse events.
items.significance: First fully non-invasive transcranial MRgFUS BBB opening in brain-tumor patients.
items.limitations: Very small (n=5); agent change mid-study.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Incisionless FUS removes LITT's minimally invasive edge over open surgery for drug delivery (strategic headwind).
items.whyMatters: Incisionless FUS removes LITT's minimally invasive edge over open surgery for drug delivery (strategic headwind).

items.title: Safety and Feasibility of Repeated and Transient BBB Disruption by Pulsed Ultrasound in Recurrent Glioblastoma (SonoCloud-1, Idbaih)
items.citation: Idbaih A, Canney M, Belin L, et al. Clin Cancer Res. 2019;25(13):3793-3801.
items.results: BBB disruption in 52/65 sonications; transient, manageable adverse events.
items.significance: Confirms repeated monthly BBB opening is tolerable.
items.limitations: Single-center, single-arm, not efficacy-powered.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Reinforces the FUS drug-delivery track as a slowly maturing alternative (headwind).
items.whyMatters: Reinforces the FUS drug-delivery track as a slowly maturing alternative (headwind).

items.title: A Randomized Trial of Focused Ultrasound Thalamotomy for Essential Tremor (Exablate Neuro pivotal) + FDA approval
items.citation: Elias WJ, Lipsman N, Ondo WG, et al. N Engl J Med. 2016;375(8):730-739. (FDA approval July 2016).
items.endpoints: Change in hand-tremor score at 3 months
items.results: ~47% improvement vs negligible sham; basis for FDA approval of incisionless MRgFUS thalamotomy.
items.significance: Establishes MRgFUS thermal ablation as an FDA-approved incisionless brain-ablation modality.
items.limitations: Functional (not oncology) indication; skull density ratio limits eligibility.
items.clinicalBottomLine: The platform most conceptually competitive with LITT - incisionless thermal ablation - gains an FDA foothold (future headwind).
items.whyMatters: The platform most conceptually competitive with LITT - incisionless thermal ablation - gains an FDA foothold (future headwind).

items.title: Proton radiotherapy vs intensity-modulated radiotherapy for newly diagnosed glioblastoma: a phase II randomized trial
items.citation: Brown PD, Mohan R, et al. Neuro-Oncology. 2021;23(8):1337-1347.
items.studyDesign: Prospective phase II randomized (proton vs photon IMRT) with temozolomide
items.endpoints: Time to cognitive failure; PFS; OS
items.results: No difference in PFS, OS, or time to cognitive failure; protons reduced normal-tissue dose only.
items.significance: Protons did not show clinical superiority over IMRT for GBM.
items.limitations: Modest size; cognitive endpoint underpowered.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Proton therapy did not establish superiority over IMRT in GBM - neutral backdrop for LITT positioning.
items.evidenceStrength: Prospective phase II randomized (proton vs photon IMRT) with temozolomide
items.whyMatters: Proton therapy did not establish superiority over IMRT in GBM - neutral backdrop for LITT positioning.
items.changeNote: Verified: Brown/Mohan et al., Neuro-Oncology 2021;23(8):1337-1347 (PMID 33647972); no significant difference vs IMRT.

items.title: GT Medical Technologies closes oversubscribed $100M Series E to commercialize GammaTile and advance GBM (BRIDGES) trial
items.citation: GT Medical Technologies Completes Oversubscribed $100M Series E Equity Financing (PR Newswire, Jun 2026); MedTech Dive, MassDevice.

items.population: N/A; pipeline targets operable brain metastases and newly diagnosed GBM
items.results: Oversubscribed $100M Series E led by Viking Global with MVM, Gilde, Evidity, Medtech Venture Partners, FemHealth; funds GammaTile commercial expansion post-ROADS and the BRIDGES GBM RCT.

items.whyMatters: Competitive funding signal in LITT's resection-adjunct market.

items.title: NeuroOne reports Q2 FY2026 results — OneRF ablation momentum (72% product revenue growth; Zimmer Biomet distribution; UMN epilepsy collaboration)

items.title: uniQure AMT-130 (Huntington's) clears FDA path to accelerated-approval BLA — delivered via ClearPoint MRI-guided stereotactic platform
items.citation: uniQure regulatory update / SEC 8-K; STAT News, NeurologyLive; ClearPoint Neuro IR. 2026-06-17.
items.studyDesign: FDA Type B meeting outcome / regulatory milestone
items.population: Huntington's disease (Phase I/II AMT-130); delivery via ClearPoint navigation + SmartFlow cannula
items.results: FDA agreed 3-year Phase I/II AMT-130 data can be the primary basis for a BLA under accelerated approval (high-dose: 75% slowing of cUHDRS progression vs matched controls, p=0.003); BLA planned Q3 2026.
items.significance: Validates MRI-guided stereotactic intracranial delivery as a regulated, approvable platform and strengthens ClearPoint's cranial franchise (adjacent to/enabling LITT workflows).

items.clinicalBottomLine: A first potential approved therapy delivered through ClearPoint hardware de-risks a key navigation/delivery enabler and reinforces momentum for MRI-guided minimally invasive intracranial procedures.

items.whyMatters: Ecosystem validation + financial de-risking of ClearPoint.

items.title: Zongertinib in HER2-mutant NSCLC with active brain metastases (Beamion LUNG-1) - NEJM publication
items.citation: Beamion LUNG-1 investigators. First-Line Zongertinib in Advanced HER2-Mutant NSCLC. N Engl J Med 2026.
items.studyDesign: Phase Ib open-label single-arm (cohort-based), dedicated active brain-met cohort
items.population: Treatment-naive HER2-mutant advanced NSCLC with active brain metastases
items.sampleSize: Frontline n=74; active brain-met cohort n=30
items.results: Systemic ORR 76%, mPFS 14.4 mo; active brain mets intracranial ORR 47% (59% in RT-naive measurable subset), intracranial PFS 8.2 mo.
items.significance: A well-tolerated, CNS-active oral HER2 TKI that can control intracranial disease without upfront local therapy in a histology that brain-metastasizes often.
items.limitations: Single-arm early-phase; small CNS cohort; accelerated-approval evidence base.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Highly CNS-active oral HER2 TKI may defer local ablative therapy (incl. LITT) in HER2-mutant NSCLC brain mets.
items.evidenceStrength: Phase Ib single-arm; CNS cohort verified
items.competitiveImpact: CNS-active systemic therapy can defer upfront local therapy in HER2-mutant NSCLC brain mets.
items.whyMatters: Systemic CNS control competes with local ablation in a molecular subset.

items.title: FDA accepts NDA for Pixclara (TLX101-Px, 18F-FET) amino-acid PET for recurrent/progressive glioma
items.citation: Telix Pharmaceuticals press release / SEC 6-K; OncLive, CancerNetwork. 2026-04-09.
items.results: FDA accepted the NDA for 18F-FET PET imaging agent (Orphan Drug + Fast Track); PDUFA goal date 2026-09-11; intended to distinguish tumor progression from treatment-related change.
items.significance: Moves a guideline-endorsed amino-acid PET tracer toward US approval - directly targets the recurrence-vs-treatment-effect question that gates LITT referral.
items.limitations: Acceptance != approval; prior CRL; PDUFA pending.
items.clinicalBottomLine: US amino-acid PET for glioma is a regulatory step closer (decision Sep 2026), sharpening selection for ablation/LITT.
items.competitiveImpact: Better recurrence-vs-necrosis discrimination improves LITT patient selection.
items.whyMatters: Improves the selection imaging upstream of LITT referral.

items.title: RAD101 (18F, FASN-targeted) PET completes Phase 2b enrollment for brain-metastasis recurrence-vs-treatment-effect
items.citation: Radiopharm Theranostics press release (GlobeNewswire), 2026-04-16.
items.population: Brain metastases (post-SRS recurrence vs treatment effect)
items.results: Completed US Phase 2b enrollment; prior interim ~90% concordance with selective tumor uptake independent of primary histology.
items.significance: A second dedicated recurrence-vs-necrosis PET tracer nearing pivotal readout - the discrimination problem central to LITT referral.
items.limitations: Enrollment milestone, not efficacy data.
items.clinicalBottomLine: A dedicated RN-vs-recurrence PET tracer finished Phase 2b enrollment; sharper selection would support appropriate LITT/necrosis referral.
items.competitiveImpact: Improved RN-vs-recurrence discrimination sharpens selection for ablation/necrosis management.
items.whyMatters: Selection-imaging pipeline relevant to LITT referral.

items.title: INDIGO (vorasidenib) extended >3-year follow-up in IDH-mutant grade 2 glioma - ASCO 2026
items.citation: Cloughesy T, et al. Updated INDIGO efficacy/safety (vorasidenib vs placebo). ASCO 2026, Abstract 2010/2010; Servier release 2026-05-31.
items.population: Residual/recurrent IDH1/2-mutant WHO grade 2 glioma post-surgery
items.endpoints: PFS (BIRC), time to next intervention, seizures, QoL, safety
items.results: Median PFS 44.1 mo; TTNI not estimable (only 23.8% had a next-intervention event); ~72% reduction in on-treatment seizure rate; neurocognition/QoL stable; grade >=3 ALT 10.8%.
items.significance: Confirms durable, deepening benefit of the IDH inhibitor in grade 2 glioma at >3 yr; reinforces deferring chemoRT.
items.limitations: Open-ended PFS CI; long-term hepatotoxicity monitoring; benefit specific to IDH-mutant grade 2.
items.clinicalBottomLine: Long-term INDIGO data solidify vorasidenib as durable first-line maintenance for resected IDH-mutant grade 2 glioma, delaying chemoRT and reducing seizures.
items.competitiveImpact: Expands effective non-surgical management of low-grade IDH-mutant glioma; mild headwind for any local-ablation niche there.
items.whyMatters: Durable systemic option further shifts low-grade glioma away from early local therapy.
